 {"id":"wa-zwcf","title":"Policy tests: capability gates, rate limiting, redaction, approval behavior","description":"# Task: Policy tests

## Goal
Prevent regressions in safety-critical logic.

Policy regressions are high-impact because they either:
- block safe operations (user-hostile), or
- allow unsafe operations (trust-destroying)

## Testing
Unit tests must cover:
- Capability gates
  - deny SendText in AltScreen
  - deny SendText when recent_gap is true
  - require prompt_active (or RequireApproval if configured)

- Rate limiting
  - per-pane/per-action budgets
  - no runaway loops

- Redaction
  - inputs and secrets never appear in logs/audit summaries

- RequireApproval + allow-once approvals
  - RequireApproval returns a scoped allow-once payload
  - approval enables ONLY the matching action fingerprint
  - TTL expiry works
  - Deny decisions cannot be overridden via approval

- **Reservations**
  - non-owner actions are denied/require-approval per config
  - owner actions are allowed
  - expired reservations are treated as no reservation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail loudly on any policy behavior change.
- Adding a new policy rule requires adding/adjusting tests.


LABELS: area-safety, area-tests, phase-1

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-4vx.8: [EPIC] Safety & policy engine (capability gates, rate limits, redaction) ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.1: Define policy model: ActionKind, PolicyDecision, authorize() API (capabilities provided separately) ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.8: PaneCapabilities derivation: PromptActive/CommandRunning/AltScreen/RecentGap (deterministic first, safe fallback) ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.9: Approval tokens (allow-once): ergonomic override for RequireApproval decisions ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.2: Rate limiting: per-pane, per-action-kind (avoid spam / runaway loops) ● P1
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.4: Policy rules loaded from config (allow/deny/require approval) + robot-safe errors ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.1.6.2: Enforce reservations in PolicyEngine + send paths ● P2

 {"id":"wa-zz5i","title":"Consistent help patterns: examples, cross-references, unified style","description":"
# Consistent Help Patterns

## Purpose
Make help text comprehensive, consistent, and genuinely helpful.

## Help Structure
Every command has:
1. One-line description
2. Usage synopsis
3. Options with descriptions
4. Examples (most important!)
5. See Also (related commands)

## Template
```
wa <command> - <one-line description>

USAGE:
    wa <command> [OPTIONS] [ARGS]

OPTIONS:
    -o, --option <VALUE>  Description [default: X]

EXAMPLES:
    wa <command> arg              Basic usage
    wa <command> --opt val arg    With option
    wa <command> --dry-run arg    Preview mode

SEE ALSO:
    wa related-cmd    Related functionality
    wa another        Alternative approach
```

## Implementation
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(
    about = "Send text to a pane",
    long_about = None,
    after_help = SEND_EXAMPLES,
)]
struct SendCmd { ... }

const SEND_EXAMPLES: &str = r#"
EXAMPLES:
    wa send --pane 3 "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --wait-for ">>>" "continue"

SEE ALSO:
    wa status       List available panes
    wa why          Explain send failures
"#;
```

## Validation
CI check ensures:
- All commands have help text
- All commands have examples
- No broken cross-references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands follow consistent help template
- [ ] All commands have at least 2 examples
- [ ] See Also references are accurate
- [ ] CI validates help text completeness

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

 {"id":"wa-zzo1","title":"[Human command] `wa workflow` (run workflow manually; show step logs/progress)","description":"# Task: Human command wa workflow

## Goal
Allow humans to manually run a workflow against a pane for debugging/recovery.

## Command examples
- wa workflow run handle_compaction <pane_id>
- wa workflow run handle_usage_limits <pane_id> --force
- wa workflow show <execution_id>

## Workspace/config
- --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
- --config (optional)

## UX
- TTY: show step-by-step progress as it runs (or tail step logs if running asynchronously).
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Safety
- Same policy gates as workflows run automatically.
- Surface policy decisions clearly.

## Testing
- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (args/exit codes/JSON stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - core workflow behaviors are validated by E2E scripts under `wa-4vx.10.*` (compaction, usage limits, resume-after-restart).

## Logging & debuggability
- The command output must always include (TTY or JSON):
  - `workflow_execution_id`
  - final outcome (success/aborted/denied) + reason
  - a short per-step summary (waits/sends) with redaction

This makes “what happened?” answerable without digging in DB tables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A human can run a workflow and see a clear, minimal summary of:
  - steps executed
  - waits/timeouts
  - sends performed (redacted)
  - final outcome

+{"id":"ft-r4l82","title":"Mock-code-finder sweep of mux/term/codec: 0 production stubs, ft-yv02l #6-7 re-triage","description":"Multi-method mock-code-finder sweep of frankenterm/mux (25 files), frankenterm/term (26 files), frankenterm/codec (5 files) on 2026-04-20.

## Methods
- Keyword grep: TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX/STUB/PLACEHOLDER/MOCK/DUMMY/FAKE/TEMP
- unimplemented!() / todo!() scan
- Short-function/fallback-return scan: -> bool { true/false }, -> Option<_> { None }, empty bodies
- Hedge-phrase grep: not yet, for now, best effort, no-op

## Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Verdict |
|---|----------|----------|---------|
| 1-29 | mux/src/pane.rs:603-775 + mux/src/tab.rs:4027-5100+ | 29x unimplemented!() in FakePane test doubles | False positive - inside #[cfg(test)] modules (pane.rs:595, tab.rs:4004). Legitimate Rust pattern: test doubles only impl trait methods the tests exercise. |
| 30 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:141 | // TODO: selective_erase when supported (struct-field comment) | Documentation of known terminal-emulator limitation. Not a stub - the struct works without this field. |
| 31 | term/src/terminalstate/mod.rs:1251 | // TODO: see https://vt100.net/docs/vt510-rm/DECSTR.html in Device::SoftReset | Reference comment. The SoftReset handler is fully implemented below - comment is just a spec pointer. |
| 32-33 | term/src/test/c0.rs:13,36 | // TODO: when we can set the left margin, we should test that here | Test-coverage gap. Tests still run; two cases arent covered because the test framework doesnt expose left-margin setup. |
| 34 | term/src/test/mod.rs:9 | // mod selection; FIXME: port to render layer | Historical module disable. The selection test module was moved to the render layer; this comment is a bread-crumb. |
| 35 | mux/src/ssh.rs:957 | fn process_id(&self) -> Option<u32> { None } | Correct by design - WrappedSshChild has no local PID because the child process runs on the remote SSH host. None is the honest answer. |
| 36 | mux/src/ssh.rs:962 | fn as_raw_handle(&self) -> Option<RawHandle> { None } (Windows) | Same rationale as #35. |

## Corrects prior record

The closed bead ft-yv02l (2026-04-20 'Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep') listed the mux tab.rs and pane.rs unimplemented!() clusters under 'Cat 2' and deferred them as 'out-of-scope, pane 1 (mux) territory' requiring domain-owner review. That classification was incorrect - these are test scaffolding inside #[cfg(test)] modules. There is no pane-1-owner action required.

## Outcome

- 0 production stubs
- 0 actionable TODOs (all 5 are documentation / test-coverage / historical disables)
- 0 commits required
- Memory note recorded for future sweeps

## Why this bead exists

Audit trail so future /mock-code-finder passes over the frankenterm/mux|term|codec cluster can skip the scan (its mature WezTerm-heritage code) and correct the residual noise from ft-yv02l #6-7.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:33:07.647071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416510Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:58:39.416201Z","close_reason":"Verified ft-yv02l #6-7 are #[cfg(test)] FakePane scaffolding only; no production stub to fix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
